description: Foxtrot is a Swedish criminal network that emerged in the late 2010s, primarily involved in narcotics distribution. Led by Rawa Majid, also known as the Kurdish Fox, the network has developed direct operational ties with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security. It is linked to violent crimes including shootings, bombings, and attacks on Israeli and Jewish targets in Europe. The network recruits vulnerable individuals and has been a major concern for Swedish law enforcement.

country_links.evidence_quote: Foxtrot has developed direct operational ties with the Iranian government , specifically Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS).
country_links.context: Foxtrot maintains direct operational ties with the Iranian government and carries out attacks on Israeli and Jewish targets across Europe under Iranian direction.

country_links.evidence_quote: Iraqi authorities announced the arrest of leadership figures associated with the Sweden-based Foxtrot Network.
country_links.context: Foxtrot leadership figures were arrested in Iraq, indicating the network's presence there.

country_links.evidence_quote: The man is alleged to have connections to other criminal organizations in Europe and to have been involved in the importation of significant amounts of drugs from the Netherlands
country_links.context: A person connected to Foxtrot was involved in importing significant amounts of drugs from the Netherlands.

country_links.evidence_quote: involved in the importation of significant amounts of drugs from the Netherlands , Sweden and to Norway .
country_links.context: A person connected to Foxtrot was involved in importing drugs to Norway.

country_links.evidence_quote: Majid, along with other Foxtrot members, has purchased Turkish citizenship in order to avoid Swedish police whilst being able to control the network from Turkey.
country_links.context: Foxtrot members, including the leader, have purchased Turkish citizenship and control the network from Turkey.
